task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Inventory configuration page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Magento Admin panel is visible, and the user is on Stores > Configuration > Catalog > Inventory.

task_completion.description: Agent sets 'Display Out of Stock Products' to 'Yes'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under Stock Options, the 'Display Out of Stock Products' dropdown is set to 'Yes'. The 'Use system value' checkbox next to it must be unchecked.

task_completion.description: Agent sets 'Only X left Threshold' to 5.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under Stock Options, the 'Only X left Threshold' input field contains the value '5'. The 'Use system value' checkbox next to it must be unchecked.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Catalog configuration page and expands the Product Alerts section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user is on Stores > Configuration > Catalog > Catalog, and the 'Product Alerts' or 'Product Alerts Run Settings' section is expanded.

task_completion.description: Agent enables stock and price alerts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Allow Alert When Product Price Changes' and 'Allow Alert When Product Comes Back in Stock' dropdowns are both set to 'Yes'. The 'Use system value' checkboxes must be unchecked.

task_completion.description: Agent sets the email identity to Customer Support.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The email sender/identity dropdown for product alerts (e.g., 'Alert Email Sender') is set to 'Customer Support'.
The 'Save Config' button is clicked, and a success message such as 'You saved the configuration.' is displayed at the top of the page.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser to interact with the Magento Admin panel.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Magento web GUI rather than using terminal commands (like bin/magento config:set) to change settings.

integrity.description: The screenshots show a genuine Magento 2 Admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches Magento 2 Admin styling, menus, and configuration layouts, not a mock page or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a database client to directly edit the core_config_data table.
integrity.visual_evidence: No screenshots of phpMyAdmin, MySQL CLI, or other database management tools being used to insert or update configuration values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring Magento 2 via the Admin panel. In Stores > Configuration > Catalog > Inventory, 'Display Out of Stock Products' should be 'Yes' and 'Only X left Threshold' should be '5'. In Stores > Configuration > Catalog > Catalog > Product Alerts, 'Allow Alert When Product Comes Back in Stock' and 'Allow Alert When Product Price Changes' should be 'Yes'. The email identity for alerts ('Alert Email Sender') should be set to 'Customer Support'. Note: 'Use system value' checkboxes must be unchecked to modify these settings.
